issues.objection: Source pack for 7.30 captures Devanagari from vedabase.io (raw HTML scrape, byte-identical), IAST from the same source, anvaya derived from the synonyms table, and verbatim Shankara/Ramanuja excerpts under fair-use clipping (≤300 chars) with full copyright holders cited (Advaita Ashrama, Sri Ramakrishna Math). Engineering layer keeps the verse's central Sanskrit terms (yoga-māyā, dvandva-moha, brahman, adhyātma, adhibhūta, adhidaiva, adhiyajña, prayāṇa-kāle) without translating them away. Cross-tradition disagreements logged with explanations. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Chapter-closing verse. Heavy metaphysics: adhibhūta/adhidaiva/adhiyajña + prayāṇa-kāla. Forward-pointer to Ch 8 honored explicitly. Engineering analog scoped at career-end-departure substrate-articulation. Counter-example: performative all-hands talk that does not survive the team's actual problems is the failure mode.

notes: Source pack for 7.30 carries verbatim quotes from Prabhupada (Gaudiya), editorial (multi-sampradaya), Shankara (Advaita, via Gambhirananda), and Ramanuja (Vishishtadvaita, via Adidevananda). literal_meaning and traditional_meaning_consensus reflect the convergence; engineering layer remains anchored in the same convergence. PASS.
